metadata.description: April 13 2026 session: processed morning meeting transcript with Ariya, applied figure-by-figure feedback (Figure 1 topic coloring + 50% HDR fix, Figure 2 top-3 annotation + legend reposition, Figure 4 rebuilt with corrected Panel E and data-driven Panel D, Figure S1 density contours, Figure S2 upper-right legend). Also built canonical figure_cache/ to eliminate PCA drift across figure scripts and updated HANDOFF.

summary: Stale session from 2026-04-13 figure/abstract work, closed without further edits because the 2026-04-14 literature-review sweep did not log into it. New session started for manuscript reference cleanup (drop Mohr, drop Törnberg, rework Crawford) + pending surgical edits (H1/H2/H3 prose conversion, Results bullet→prose, policy bullet tightening, .docx regen). See HANDOFF_NEXT_SESSION.md for full state.

annotation.content: Session started mid-workflow. Substantial figure work was already completed earlier in this same conversation before TRACE logging began: (a) processed ai-art_20260412_093634 meeting transcript with Ariya, (b) Figure 2 top-3 concentration annotation added, (c) Figure S1 converted from convex hulls to 50% density contours, (d) Figure 4 rebuilt as scripts/regenerate_figure_4.py with corrected Panel E (y=x proportional-representation line, no deficit arrows) and Panel D recomputed from Lovato data (caught utility-consensus bug: old PNG showed 66%, actual is 46%), (e) Figure 1 rebuilt as scripts/regenerate_figure_1.py with topic-colored points and matched 50% HDR fill/line, (f) Figure 1 + variants legend fixes (T-prefix, upper-right, topic legends), (g) Figure 2 legend repositioned to clear top bars, (h) build_figure_cache.py created to eliminate PCA drift across figure scripts (canonical PC1=66.57%, PC2=14.25%), (i) captions updated in both manuscripts + both ANONYMOUS versions, (j) abstract_alt_draft.md proposed but NOT committed (Oliver said he wanted to do that edit himself), (k) 191/191 tests still passing. Per TRACE absolute rule (never fabricate timing), those events are NOT being backfilled as individual timestamped events — they are summarized in this single retrospective annotation. Going forward in this session, decisions/contributions/corrections will be logged live.

contribution.description: Wrote TRACE/docs/auto-start-failure-analysis-2026-04-13.md — a field incident report on why TRACE auto-start failed during today's when-algorithms-meet-artists session. Covers: (1) TRACE tools being deferred requiring ToolSearch before use, (2) harness reminding about TaskCreate but not TRACE, (3) CLAUDE.md as weak trigger for session start, (4) ambiguous workflow boundary in user's prompt. Separates session-specific diagnosis from general hook/rule design (which belongs in TRACE repo itself).

context.conversation_snippet: can you put this info about what TRACE needs to do to improve (whether hook or whatever) and make a doc of it and put it in the TRACE dir on this machine?

decision.description: Generate an alternative Figure S2 using 50% HDR density contours per artist concern theme (matching the Figure 1 / Figure S1 rendering style) instead of 95% convex hulls. Keep the original hull version intact as figure_S2_artist_concern_themes.png; new version saved alongside as figure_S2_density.png.
decision.rationale: Consistency across semantic-map figures. Figure 1 and Figure S1 both use 50% HDR density contours; only Figure S2 still uses convex hulls. User wants to compare a density-contour version before deciding which to submit. Data source is identical (cached art2d + theme labels); only the visual aggregation changes.

context.conversation_snippet: lets make an alternative version of figure S2 where it uses the same type of density contours or whatever thye are that the other figures of the semantic map use (figures 1 and S1)

contribution.description: Created scripts/regenerate_figure_s2_density.py (alt Figure S2 rendering using 50% HDR density contours per artist theme) that matches the visual language of Figure 1 and Figure S1. Same cached art2d + theme_labels_art inputs as the canonical hull version; only the visual aggregation differs. Output saved as figure_S2_density alongside the hull version so the two can be compared.
Variant E centroid labels: fontsize 6.5→8.5, color white→#111111 (near-black), base marker size 60→110. Labels now readable at normal viewing distance against the red/blue centroid fills.

context.conversation_snippet: for figure 1 variant E, the font for the "T*" that labels each topic needs to be slightly larger and in darker font so it is easy to read

contribution.description: Variant E T-labels moved from centered-on-marker to just-below-marker: xytext=(0, -11) pts, va="top". Text now reads on white background instead of the colored marker fill.

context.conversation_snippet: move it so that the text labels sit just under the points they are labeling actually

decision.description: Commit Draft A (~6 numbers) of the lighter abstract to all 4 manuscript files (NatCol + NMS, named + ANONYMOUS) and verify Supplementary Information references match the updated figures (Figure 1 topic coloring, Figure S1 density contours). Keep canonical Figure S2 as the convex-hull version; the density S2 alternative remains as a parallel file pending user pick.
decision.rationale: User said "update the manuscript with the improved abstract." Two lighter drafts exist (A: ~6 numbers, B: ~4 numbers). Draft A is closer to Oliver's meeting framing of "nothing major, just less number dense" — it keeps the 252-artists scale anchor and the 99.9%/4-of-20 finding while pruning secondary percentages. Draft B is more aggressive. Going with A as the safer commit; user can swap to B if preferred.
decision.revision_note: User said "now update the manuscript with the improved abstract." Committed Draft A across all 4 manuscript MD files. SI required no abstract change. .docx files regenerated via pandoc. Tests: 191/191 still passing.

context.conversation_snippet: now update the manuscript with the improved abstract and with these updated figures. do same for the supp info

contribution.description: Committed lighter abstract (Draft A) to all 4 manuscript files: NatCol named, NatCol ANONYMOUS, NMS named, NMS ANONYMOUS. Word count 237 → 224. Numerical tokens reduced from ~12 (1259, 99.9%, 4, 20, 15, 58.2%, 6, 30.6%, 24, 3, 504) to 5 (2013, 2025, 252, 4, 20). Narrative arc and "low-pass filter" framing preserved. Body of manuscript retains all original specific percentages as evidence; only the abstract was lightened. SI files (NatCol + NMS) verified — no stale figure references, PC values already at canonical 66.6/14.2. All 6 .docx files (manuscripts + SI for both venues) regenerated via pandoc. Test suite 191/191 passing.

context.conversation_snippet: now update the manuscript with the improved abstract and with these updated figures. do same for the supp info

decision.description: Create a labeled variant of Figure 1 that overlays T* centroid labels on every topic, while preserving the canonical figure_1_semantic_density.png unchanged. New file: figure_1_semantic_density_labeled.png. Same cache-loaded coords + same 50% HDR + same legend; only addition is a per-topic centroid label at each topic's 2D mean position.
decision.rationale: Reader can visually link a colored point cluster on the map back to its T* identity in the legend. The canonical Figure 1 stays untouched so reviewer-facing submission isn't affected by this experimental view.

context.conversation_snippet: without deleting anything, i need you to make another verison of figure 1 that includes the "T*" labels to each of the topics to clearly identify each.

contribution.description: Created scripts/regenerate_figure_1_labeled.py — labeled variant of canonical Figure 1 with T* centroid labels at each topic's 2D mean position. Labels in white-bg boxes outlined in the topic's color so they read against the colored point cloud. Saved as figure_1_semantic_density_labeled.png alongside the canonical version (canonical untouched).

context.conversation_snippet: without deleting anything, i need you to make another verison of figure 1 that includes the "T*" labels to each of the topics to clearly identify each.

decision.description: Replace abstract with a third revision that (a) removes "probes" entirely, (b) leads with the more striking 2-of-20 / ~95% concentration finding instead of 4-of-20 / 99.9%, (c) names the 24-to-1 ownership compression explicitly, (d) makes the second half follow narratively from the first by linking style-matching controls directly to "rules out a writing-register explanation."
decision.rationale: Oliver's feedback on Draft A: dry/jargony, "probes" undefined for cold readers, second half unclear, missing actual compression numbers. Concentration framing changes from "99.9% in 4 topics" to "nearly 95% in 2 topics" because T13 (51.1%) + T1 (43.8%) = 94.9% — a starker statement of the same finding.
decision.revision_note: Abstract rewritten and committed across all 4 manuscript MD files + .docx regenerated. Verification: 233 words, no "probes", "95%" + "2 of 20" present, "24-to-1" present. The compression number is now explicit. Second-half flow improved by linking style-matching controls directly to the writing-register explanation it rules out.

context.conversation_snippet: extremely important, is that the abstract does not flow well at all! ... avoid using "probes" at all in the abstract because those need to be defined before they are understood. ... nearly 95% of artist probes fall into just 2 of the 20 topics ... no mention of the actual compression numbers!

contribution.description: Third abstract revision committed. Key changes from Draft A: (1) "probes" removed entirely → "statements" / "voice" / "survey responses"; (2) headline finding flipped from "99.9% in 4 topics" to "Nearly 95% in just 2 of 20 topics" — same data, sharper framing (T13 51.1% + T1 43.8% = 94.9%); (3) compression number now explicit: "24 distinct ownership positions ... a 24-to-1 compression"; (4) "structural, not stylistic, persisting after matched style controls" rewritten to "persists after style-matching controls and rules out a writing-register explanation" so the cold reader knows what was controlled and why; (5) "Compression also operates inside" connector phrase makes the H2→H3 transition explicit. Word count 233. Numerical tokens 9 (2013, 2025, 252, 95%, 2, 20, 24, 24, 1) — slightly more than Draft A's 5 because the compression numbers are now in the abstract.

context.conversation_snippet: extremely important, is that the abstract does not flow well at all! ... avoid using "probes" at all in the abstract because those need to be defined before they are understood. ... nearly 95% of artist probes fall into just 2 of the 20 topics ... no mention of the actual compression numbers!

decision.description: Conduct deep research analysis comparing the current Nature Collection manuscript writing style to ~10-15 recent (2023-2025) related publications across computational social science, AI governance, AI ethics, and the specific PALCOMMS "Reframing AI" guest collection. Analyze writing style, jargon use/avoidance, metaphor patterns at abstract / intro / results / discussion levels. Report comparison + recommendations. NO MANUSCRIPT EDITS at this stage.
decision.rationale: User wants the manuscript writing style benchmarked against the venue and adjacent literature before final pass. Avoid editing-without-grounding. Need to know what jargon/metaphor norms the target audience expects.

context.conversation_snippet: i need you to review several recent and relevant publications and preprints that relate to this type of work and the Nature Collection that we are going to submit to. we need to get a sense of how abstracts are written and, more generally, how the entire manuscripts are written for related work.

decision.description: Proceed with Option A (editor-aligned rewrite): pull manuscript register toward Mead & Barbosa Neves's qualitative-theoretical pattern. Steps: (1) sample 2-3 more recent papers by the editors to calibrate, (2) count current references vs. venue norm, (3) rewrite abstract along Option A lines, (4) drop H1/H2/H3 hypothesis numbering throughout, (5) replace rhetorical-question opening, (6) reduce competing metaphors to one structuring system (compression + four mechanisms).
decision.rationale: Synthesis report compared current manuscript to 22 papers (PALCOMMS + adjacent). Option A optimizes for the actual editors' register (theory-forward, qualitative findings with anchor numbers, named-construct hinge, prescription-deferred close). Option B (Nature HB / Lee et al. style) was the alternative.
decision.revision_note: Option A first wave executed: (1) abstract rewritten to editor-aligned register (252 words, named-construct-as-hinge with italicized *semantic compression*, qualitative findings with scope-anchor numbers only, no "low-pass filter," UK spelling); (2) intro opening rewritten from rhetorical question to declarative phenomenon; (3) all 4 manuscript MD files updated; (4) all 4 .docx regenerated. Reference count gap (~20-40 short of PALCOMMS empirical norm) flagged for separate decision. H1/H2/H3 markers, intro subheading metaphors, and results bullet-to-prose conversion remain pending.

context.conversation_snippet: lets go ahead and grab a few more examples of Mead and Neves work and then proceed with the recommended option A. then also tell me how our reference count is vs what is expected or typical etc.

contribution.description: Option A first-wave commits across 4 manuscript MD files and 4 .docx files: abstract rewritten editor-aligned (252 words, italicized *semantic compression* construct, qualitative findings, UK spelling), intro opening switched from rhetorical question to declarative phenomenon. Verified: abstract contains italic construct, omits "low-pass filter," uses scope-anchor numbers only.

context.conversation_snippet: lets go ahead and grab a few more examples of Mead and Neves work and then proceed with the recommended option A. then also tell me how our reference count is vs what is expected or typical etc.

contribution.description: Completed reference verification + placement parallel sweep: 16 single-reference verification agents + 1 methods-references finder + 1 placement strategist. Output: critical assessments of each candidate, ranked by substantive vs performative, with specific manuscript insertion points and surrounding-text edits.

context.conversation_snippet: i need you to create a subagent for each and every one of those suggested references, along with another subagent that finds the other references you suggested finding for the methods etc, and confirms how and why each one relates to our manuscript. then have another agent or set of agents go through and determine where exactly these references would be added to the manuscript and what parts of the manuscript they would change.

contribution.description: Wrote next-session handoff plan at the top of HANDOFF_NEXT_SESSION.md. Governing principle: every citation must do real work, no performance. Plan executes the self-audit conclusions: drop Mohr et al. + Törnberg & Uitermark (performative), rework Crawford to engage her classification-politics chapter specifically (which also foreshadows Bowker & Star later). Skip all three queue items (Bender, Broussard, Benjamin) as filler. Plus execute the deprioritised edits: H1/H2/H3 removal in Results, bullet-to-prose conversion, map/territory subheading consolidation (flag to user), and policy-bullet tightening. Then regenerate .docx and verify 191/191 tests. Includes context on decisions not to reverse and TRACE pickup guidance.

context.conversation_snippet: i want you to write out a plan for the next claude code agent to come in and pick up from where you left off. i want to use the references that are doing real work rather than performing depth. no performance - only quality since this is going to be a journal submission ASAP.
